Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/5/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short-answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short-answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long-answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question 14 is a case study based question with two subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the vector equation of a line passing through a point with position vector {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and parallel to the line joining the points {tex}-\hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}.

 2
2.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex}

 2
3.

Find the general solution for differential equation:  {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}

 2
4.(i)

Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.

 2

OR
(ii)

A box B1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B1 and B2, then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour.

 2
5.

Let X be a random variable which assumes values x1, x2, x3, x4 such that 2P (X = x1) = 3P (X = x2) = P (X = x3) = 5P (X = x4). Find the probability distribution of X.

 2
6.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}

 2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} 

 3
8.

Find the equation of the plane passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 10 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}})+4{/tex} = 0 and passing through the point (-2, 3, 1).

 3
9.(i)

Let {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. If {tex}\hat{n}{/tex} is a unit vector such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0, then find {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are unit vectors inclined at an angle 30o to each other, then find the area of the parallelogram with {tex}(\vec{a}+3 \vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} as adjacent sides.

 3
10.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex}

 3

SECTION C

Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.(i)

Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin2x)dx + tan x dy = 0.

 4

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x3 + y3)dy = x2ydx

 4
12.

Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration.

 4
13.

In a factory, machine A produces 30% of the total output, machine B produces 25% and the machine C produces the remaining output. If defective items produced by machines A, B and C are 1%, 1.2%, 2% respectively. Three machines working together produce 10000 items in a day. An item is drawn at random from a day's output and found to be defective. Find the probability that it was produced by machine B?

 4

Case Study Based Question
14.

Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find the shortest distance between the given lines.

 2
(ii)

Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide.

 2

Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/5/2 (set-2)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.

  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. This question paper contains three Sections - A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section-A has 6 short-answer type-I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section-B has 4 short-answer type-II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section-C has 4 long-answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question 14 is a case study based question with two subparts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the vector equation of a line passing through a point with position vector {tex}2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and parallel to the line joining the points {tex}-\hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} and {tex}\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}{/tex}.

Solution:

Let A = {tex}2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow B=-\widehat i+4\widehat j+\widehat k,\overrightarrow C=\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{BC}=\overrightarrow C-\overrightarrow B{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{BC}=\widehat i+2\widehat j+2\widehat k+\widehat i-4\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex}
{tex}\overrightarrow{BC}=2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}
We know that the equation of a line passing through a point vector a and parallel to b is
r = a + {tex}\lambda{/tex}b.
Here, vector a = 2i - j + k
So, the equation of the required line
{tex}\overrightarrow r=2\widehat i-\widehat j+\widehat k+\lambda\left(2\widehat i-2\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex}

 2
2.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex}
Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt
{tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex}
So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex}
Putting value of t
{tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex}

 2
3.

Find the general solution for differential equation:  {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}

Solution:

We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex}
Integrating both the sides we get:
{tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex}
{tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex}

 2
4.(i)

Events A and B are such that P(A) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} and {tex}\mathrm{P}(\bar{\mathrm{A}} \cup \bar{\mathrm{B}})=\frac{1}{4}{/tex} Find whether the events A and B are independent or not.

Solution:

P(A) = {tex}\frac12{/tex}, P(B) = {tex}\frac7{12}{/tex}, {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=\frac14{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}P\left(A'\cup B'\right)=P\left(A\cap B\right)'{/tex}
{tex}\frac14=1-P\left(A\cap B\right){/tex}
{tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)=1-\frac14=\frac34{/tex}
Also, P(A).P(B) = {tex}\frac12×\frac7{12}=\frac7{24}{/tex}
Clearly ​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​, {tex}P\left(A\cap B\right)\neq P\left(A\right).P\left(B\right){/tex}​​​​​​​
{tex}\because{/tex}​​​​​​​ the Events A and B are independent 

 2

OR
(ii)

A box B1 contains 1 white ball and 3 red balls. Another box B2 contains 2 white balls and 3 red balls. If one ball is drawn at random from each of the boxes B1 and B2, then find the probability that the two balls drawn are of the same colour.

Solution:

{tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)=\frac14,\;P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)=\frac34{/tex}
{tex}P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)=\frac25,\;P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right)=\frac35{/tex}
Probability that both the balls drawn from box B1 and B2 each of same colour
= {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)+P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac14\times\frac25+\frac34\times\frac35=\frac2{20}+\frac9{20}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{11}{20}{/tex}

 2
5.

Let X be a random variable which assumes values x1, x2, x3, x4 such that 2P (X = x1) = 3P (X = x2) = P (X = x3) = 5P (X = x4). Find the probability distribution of X.

Solution:

Let P(X = x3) = k Then,
P(x = x1) = {tex}\frac k2{/tex}
P(x = x2) = {tex}\frac k3{/tex}
P(x = x4) = {tex}\frac k5{/tex}
We know that the sum of probabilities in a probability distribution is always 1.
P(X = x​​​​​​1) + P(X = x2) + P(X = x3)+ P(X = x4) = 1
{tex}\frac K2+\frac K3+K+\frac K5=1{/tex}
{tex}\frac{15K+10K+30K+6K}{30}=1{/tex}
61K = 30
K = {tex}\frac{30}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​

X 1 2 3 4
f(X) {tex}\frac{K}2=\frac{15}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}3=\frac{10}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}K=\frac{30}{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}{5}=\frac6{61}{/tex}​​​​​​​
2
6.

If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex}, then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex}
As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex}
x + y + z = 1 ...(i)
Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
On compairing
z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii)
z = y
Putting in eqn (i)
x + 2y = 1
-x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii))
y = 0, z = 0, x = 1
So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex}
{tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex}

 2

SECTION B

Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Taking sin2x as first function
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex}
Now, from eqn (i)
{tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex}
{tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} 

Solution:

Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex}.
Put, x2 = t  {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain
I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} 
Using partial fractions,
 {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii)
Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1
Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1.
Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain
{tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex}   I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x2 + 1| - log |x2 + 2| + C

 3
8.

Find the equation of the plane passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 10 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}})+4{/tex} = 0 and passing through the point (-2, 3, 1).

Solution:

{tex}\vec r.\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)=10{/tex} and {tex}\vec r.\left(2\hat i+3\hat j-\hat k\right)=-4{/tex} point (-2, 3, 1) is given
So, {tex}n_1=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\;n_2=2\hat i+3\hat j-\hat k{/tex}
d1 = 10, d2 = -4
Now, equation of plane
{tex}\vec r.\left(\vec{n_1}+\lambda\vec{n_2}\right)=d_1+\lambda d_2{/tex}
{tex}\vec r.\left(\hat i+\vec j+\vec k+2\lambda\hat i+3\lambda\hat j-\lambda\hat k\right)=10-4\lambda{/tex}
{tex}\vec r.\left[\left(1+2\lambda\right)\hat i+\left(1+3\lambda\right)\hat j+\left(1+\lambda\right)\hat k\right]=10-4\lambda{/tex}
Put {tex}\vec r=\left(-2\hat i+3\hat j+\hat k\right){/tex}
{tex}\left(-2\hat i+3\hat j+\hat k\right).\left[\left(1+2\lambda\right)\hat i+\left(1+3\lambda\right)\hat j+\left(1+\lambda\right)\hat k\right]{/tex} {tex}= 10 - 4\lambda{/tex}
{tex}-2-4\lambda+9\lambda+3+1-\lambda{/tex} {tex}=10-4\lambda{/tex}
{tex}\lambda=1{/tex}​​​​​​​
Now, by putting its value the required equation is
{tex}\vec r.\left(3\hat i+4\hat j\right)=6{/tex}​​​​​​​

 3
9.(i)

Let {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. If {tex}\hat{n}{/tex} is a unit vector such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0, then find {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|{/tex}.

Solution:

Given {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j,\;\vec b=\hat i-\hat j,\;\vec c=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex}
{tex}\hat n=\frac{\vec a\times\vec b}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex}
So, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\1&-1&0\end{vmatrix}=-2\hat k{/tex}
∴ {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|c.\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(-2\hat k\right)\right|}{\left|-2\hat k\right|}{/tex}
{tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{2\hat k}{2\hat k}=1{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are unit vectors inclined at an angle 30o to each other, then find the area of the parallelogram with {tex}(\vec{a}+3 \vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} as adjacent sides.

Solution:

Area of a parallelogram having adjacent side as p and q = {tex}\left|\vec p\times\vec q\right|{/tex}
Area {tex}=\left|\left(\vec a+3\vec b\right)\times\left(3\vec a+\vec b\right)\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|3\left(\vec a\times\vec a\right)+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+9\left(\vec b\times\vec a\right)+3\left(\vec b\times\vec b\right)\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|3\times0+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)-9\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+3\times0\right|{/tex}
{tex}=\left|-8\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|=8\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|{/tex}
{tex}=8\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\vec b\right|\sin\theta=8\times1\times1\times\sin30^\circ{/tex}
​​​​​​​{tex}=8\times\frac12=4\:{/tex}​​​​​​​sq.unit​​​​​​​

SECTION C

Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
10.

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \frac{1}{1+(\tan x)^{\frac{2}{3}}} d x{/tex} ...(i)
Using property,
{tex}\int_\limits0^af(x)dx=\int_\limits0^af(a-x)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[\tan\left({\displaystyle\frac\pi2}-x\right)\right]^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{dx}{1+\left[cotx\right]^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex} ...(ii)
Adding eqn (i) and (ii)
{tex}2I=\int_\limits0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}\frac{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23dx}{1+\left(\tan x\right)^\frac23}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[x\right]_0^{\textstyle\frac\pi2}{/tex}
{tex}2I=\left[\frac\pi2-0\right]{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi4{/tex}

 3
11.(i)

Solve the following differential equation: (y-sin2x)dx + tan x dy = 0.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} (y - sin2x) dx + tan x dy = 0
tan x dy = -(y - sin2x)dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{(sin^2x-y)}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{sin^2x}{tanx}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{y}{tanx}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx} + ycotx = sinx \space cosx{/tex}
It is a differential equation in linear form
{tex}\therefore{/tex} P = cotx, Q = sin x {tex}\cdot{/tex} cos x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I.F = {tex}e^{\int P d x}=e^{\int \cot x d x}{/tex} = {tex}e^{\log |\sin x|}=\sin x{/tex}
So, solution is
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} IF = {tex}\int Q \cdot I F d x+C{/tex},
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sin x = sinx {tex}\cdot{/tex} cosx {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx + c,
y . sinx = {tex}\int \underbrace{\sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x}_I+c{/tex} ...(i)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int \sin ^2 x \cdot \cos x d x{/tex}
Let sinx = t, cos x dx = dt
{tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int t^2 d t=\frac{t^3}{3}+c_2{/tex}
I = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2{/tex}
Put in eqn (i)
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c_2+c_1{/tex}
y {tex}\cdot{/tex} sinx = {tex}\frac{\sin ^3 x}{3}+c{/tex}
Where (c = c1 + c2)

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (x3 + y3)dy = x2ydx

Solution:

(x3 + y3)dy = x2y dx
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{x^2y}{x^3+y^3}{/tex}
It is homogeneous differential equation
Put y = vx , {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=v+x\frac{dv}{dx}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{vx^3}{x^3+v^3x^3}{/tex}
{tex}v+x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac v{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}x\frac{dv}{dx}=\frac{v-v-v^4}{1+v^3}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1+v^3}{v^4}dv=-\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac1{v^4}dv+\int\frac1vdv=-\int\frac{dx}x{/tex}
{tex}-\frac1{3v^3}+\log\left|v\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
Put v = y/x
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|\frac yx\right|=-\log\left|x\right|+C{/tex}
{tex}-\frac{x^3}{3y^3}+\log\left|y\right|=C{/tex}
This is the required answer.

12.

Find the area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1, using integration.

Solution:


Required area = {tex}\int_\limits0^1y_1dx+\int_\limits1^2y_2dx{/tex}
= {tex}\int_\limits0^1-\left(x-1\right)dx+\int_\limits1^2\left(x-1\right)dx{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_0^1+\left[\frac{x^2}2-x\right]_1^2{/tex}
= {tex}-\left[\frac12-1\right]+\left[\frac42-2-\frac12+1\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac12+\frac12=1{/tex} sq. unit

13.

In a factory, machine A produces 30% of the total output, machine B produces 25% and the machine C produces the remaining output. If defective items produced by machines A, B and C are 1%, 1.2%, 2% respectively. Three machines working together produce 10000 items in a day. An item is drawn at random from a day's output and found to be defective. Find the probability that it was produced by machine B?

Solution:

Let A, E1, E2 and E3 denote the events that the item is defective, machine A is chosen, machine B is chosen and machine C is chosen, respectively. Therefore, we have,
P(E1) = {tex}\frac{30}{100}{/tex}
P(E2) = {tex}\frac{25}{100}{/tex}
P(E3) = {tex}\frac{45}{100}{/tex}
Now, we have,
{tex}P(\frac {A}{ E_{1}})=\frac{1}{100}{/tex}
{tex}P(\frac {A}{ E_{2}})=\frac{1.2}{100}{/tex}
{tex}P(\frac {A}{E_{3}})=\frac{2}{100}{/tex}
Using Bayes' theorem, we have,
Required probability = {tex}P(\frac {E_{1}} { A}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(A / E_{1}\right)}{P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(A / E_{1}\right)+P\left(E_{2}\right) P\left(A / E_{2}\right)+P\left(E_{3}\right) P\left(A / E_{3}\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\frac{30}{100} \times \frac{1}{100}}{\frac{30}{100} \times+\times \frac{1}{100} \frac{25}{100} \frac{1.2}{100}+\frac{45}{100} \times \frac{2}{100}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{30}{30+30+90}=\frac{30}{150}{/tex} = 0.2

Case Study Based Question
14.

Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively.

  1. Find the shortest distance between the given lines. (2)
  2. Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide. (2)
Solution:

  1. Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}=0,\overrightarrow{a_2}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k,\overrightarrow{b_2}=2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\1&2&-1\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex}
    {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex}
    {tex}\left(a_2-a_1\right)\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)\left(3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k\right){/tex}
    = 9 - 9 = 0
    Shortest distance between line is
    {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex}
    {tex}d=\left|\frac0{3\sqrt3}\right|{/tex} = 0 units.

  2. From equation of both lines, we get
    {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)+\mu\left(2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex}
    {tex}\lambda\widehat i+2\lambda\widehat j-\lambda\widehat k=\left(3+2\mu\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\mu\right)\widehat j+\mu\widehat k{/tex}
    On compairing
    {tex}\lambda=3+2\mu{/tex} ...(i)
    {tex}2\lambda=3+\mu{/tex} ...(ii)
    {tex}-\lambda=\mu{/tex} ...(iii)
    From eqn (iii) and (i)
    {tex}\mu=-1{/tex}
    From eqn (ii) and (iii)
    {tex}\lambda=1{/tex}
    {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex}
    Hence, at point x = 1, y = 2 & z = -1 both motorcycle collides.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{d x}{\sqrt{4 x-x^2}}{/tex} Solution: {tex}\int\frac1{\sqrt{4x-x^2}}dx=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x\right)}}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\sqrt{-\left(x^2-4x+4-4\right)}}dx=\int\frac1{-\left(x-2\right)^2+4}dx{/tex} {tex}=\int\frac1{\left(2\right)^2-\left(x-2\right)^2}dx{/tex} Put x - 2 = t, dx = dt {tex}=\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\int\frac{dx}{\left(a\right)^2-\left(x\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac xa\right)+C{/tex} So, {tex}\int\frac{dt}{\left(2\right)^2-\left(t\right)^2}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac t2\right)+C{/tex} Putting value of t {tex}=\sin^{-1}\left(\frac{x-2}2\right)+C{/tex} 2 3. Find the general solution for differential equation: {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} Solution: We have, {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=e^{x} e^{-y}+x^{2} e^{-y}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=e^{-y}\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x{/tex} Integrating both the sides we get: {tex}\Rightarrow \int \frac{d y}{e^{-y}}=\int\left(e^{x}+x^{2}\right) d x+c{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{3}}{3}+c{/tex} {tex}e^{y}=e^{x}+\frac{x^{2}}{3}+c{/tex} 2 4. Solution: {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)=\frac14,\;P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)=\frac34{/tex} {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)=\frac25,\;P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right)=\frac35{/tex} Probability that both the balls drawn from box B 1 and B 2 each of same colour = {tex}P\left(\frac W{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac W{B_2}\right)+P\left(\frac R{B_1}\right)\times P\left(\frac R{B_2}\right){/tex} {tex}=\frac14\times\frac25+\frac34\times\frac35=\frac2{20}+\frac9{20}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{11}{20}{/tex} 2 5. Let X be a random variable which assumes values x 1 , x 2 , x 3 , x 4 such that 2P (X = x 1 ) = 3P (X = x 2 ) = P (X = x 3 ) = 5P (X = x 4 ). Find the probability distribution of X. P(X = x ​​​​​​1 ) + P(X = x 2 ) + P(X = x 3 )+ P(X = x 4 ) = 1 {tex}\frac K2+\frac K3+K+\frac K5=1{/tex} {tex}\frac{15K+10K+30K+6K}{30}=1{/tex} 61K = 30 K = {tex}\frac{30}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ X 1 2 3 4 f(X) {tex}\frac{K}2=\frac{15}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}3=\frac{10}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}K=\frac{30}{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}\frac{K}{5}=\frac6{61}{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 2 6. If {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\hat{j}-\hat{k}{/tex} , then find {tex}|\vec{b}|{/tex} Solution: {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} Let {tex}\vec b=x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k{/tex} As, {tex}\vec a.\vec b=1{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=1{/tex} x + y + z = 1 ...(i) Also, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)\times\left(x\hat i+y\hat j+z\hat k\right)=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} {tex}\left(z-y\right)\hat i-\left(z-x\right)\hat j+\left(y-x\right)\hat k=\hat j-\hat k{/tex} On compairing z - y = 0, x - z = 1, y - x = -1 ...(ii) z = y Putting in eqn (i) x + 2y = 1 -x + y = -1 (from eqn (ii)) y = 0, z = 0, x = 1 So, {tex}\vec b=\hat i{/tex} {tex}\vec{\left|b\right|}=\sqrt1=\;1{/tex} 2 SECTION B Question numbers 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each. 7. (i) Find: {tex}\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Solution: {tex}I=\int \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \cdot \sin 2 \mathrm{x} \ d \mathrm{x}{/tex} Using integration by parts Taking sin2x as first function {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2\int\cos2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=\sin2x\int e^xdx-2(\cos2x.e^x+\int2\sin2x.e^x)dx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x.\sin2xdx-2\cos2x.e^x-\int4\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4\int\sin2x.e^xdx{/tex} Now, from eqn (i) {tex}I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)-4I\space+\ C{/tex} {tex}5I=e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+\ C{/tex} {tex}I=\frac15e^x\left(\sin2xdx-2\cos2x\right)+C{/tex} 3 OR (ii) Evaluate: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} Solution: Let I = {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{\left(x^{2}+1\right)\left(x^{2}+2\right)} d x{/tex} . Put, x 2 = t {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x dx = dt, we obtain I = {tex}\int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} Using partial fractions, {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}{/tex} ........(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1 = A(t + 2) + B(t + 1) ........(ii) Putting t = - 2 in (ii), we obtain: B = - 1 Putting t = - 1 in (ii), we obtain: A = 1. Substituting the values of A and B in (i), we obtain {tex}\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} I = {tex}\int \frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)} d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = {tex}\int\left(\frac{1}{t+1}-\frac{1}{t+2}\right) d t{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} I = log |t + 1| - log |t + 2| + C = log |x 2 + 1| - log |x 2 + 2| + C 3 8. Find the equation of the plane passing through the line of intersection of the planes {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex} = 10 and {tex}\vec{r} \cdot(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-\hat{\mathrm{k}})+4{/tex} = 0 and passing through the point (-2, 3, 1). Solution: {tex}\vec r.\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right)=10{/tex} and {tex}\vec r.\left(2\hat i+3\hat j-\hat k\right)=-4{/tex} point (-2, 3, 1) is given So, {tex}n_1=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k,\;n_2=2\hat i+3\hat j-\hat k{/tex} d 1 = 10, d 2 = -4 Now, equation of plane {tex}\vec r.\left(\vec{n_1}+\lambda\vec{n_2}\right)=d_1+\lambda d_2{/tex} {tex}\vec r.\left(\hat i+\vec j+\vec k+2\lambda\hat i+3\lambda\hat j-\lambda\hat k\right)=10-4\lambda{/tex} {tex}\vec r.\left[\left(1+2\lambda\right)\hat i+\left(1+3\lambda\right)\hat j+\left(1+\lambda\right)\hat k\right]=10-4\lambda{/tex} Put {tex}\vec r=\left(-2\hat i+3\hat j+\hat k\right){/tex} {tex}\left(-2\hat i+3\hat j+\hat k\right).\left[\left(1+2\lambda\right)\hat i+\left(1+3\lambda\right)\hat j+\left(1+\lambda\right)\hat k\right]{/tex} {tex}= 10 - 4\lambda{/tex} {tex}-2-4\lambda+9\lambda+3+1-\lambda{/tex} {tex}=10-4\lambda{/tex} {tex}\lambda=1{/tex} ​​​​​​​ Now, by putting its value the required equation is {tex}\vec r.\left(3\hat i+4\hat j\right)=6{/tex} ​​​​​​​ 3 9. (i) Let {tex}\vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}{/tex} , {tex}\vec{b}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{c}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex} . If {tex}\hat{n}{/tex} is a unit vector such that {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0 and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}{/tex} = 0, then find {tex}|\vec{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \hat{\mathrm{n}}|{/tex} . Solution: Given {tex}\vec a=\hat i+\hat j,\;\vec b=\hat i-\hat j,\;\vec c=\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} {tex}\hat n=\frac{\vec a\times\vec b}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} So, {tex}\vec a\times\vec b=\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\1&-1&0\end{vmatrix}=-2\hat k{/tex} ∴ {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|c.\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|}{\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{\left|\left(\hat i+\hat j+\hat k\right).\left(-2\hat k\right)\right|}{\left|-2\hat k\right|}{/tex} {tex}\left|\vec c.\hat n\right|=\frac{2\hat k}{2\hat k}=1{/tex} 3 OR (ii) If {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are unit vectors inclined at an angle 30 o to each other, then find the area of the parallelogram with {tex}(\vec{a}+3 \vec{b}){/tex} and {tex}(3 \vec{a}+\vec{b}){/tex} as adjacent sides. Solution: Area of a parallelogram having adjacent side as p and q = {tex}\left|\vec p\times\vec q\right|{/tex} Area {tex}=\left|\left(\vec a+3\vec b\right)\times\left(3\vec a+\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\left(\vec a\times\vec a\right)+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+9\left(\vec b\times\vec a\right)+3\left(\vec b\times\vec b\right)\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|3\times0+\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)-9\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)+3\times0\right|{/tex} {tex}=\left|-8\left(\vec a\times\vec b\right)\right|=8\left|\vec a\times\vec b\right|{/tex} {tex}=8\left|\vec a\right|.\left|\vec b\right|\sin\theta=8\times1\times1\times\sin30^\circ{/tex} ​​​​​​​ {tex}=8\times\frac12=4\:{/tex} ​​​​​​​sq.unit​​​​​​​ 3 SECTION C Question numbers 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each. 10. In a factory, machine A produces 30% of the total output, machine B produces 25% and the machine C produces the remaining output. If defective items produced by machines A, B and C are 1%, 1.2%, 2% respectively. Three machines working together produce 10000 items in a day. An item is drawn at random from a day's output and found to be defective. Find the probability that it was produced by machine B? Solution: Let A, E 1 , E 2 and E 3 denote the events that the item is defective, machine A is chosen, machine B is chosen and machine C is chosen, respectively. Therefore, we have, P(E 1 ) = {tex}\frac{30}{100}{/tex} P(E 2 ) = {tex}\frac{25}{100}{/tex} P(E 3 ) = {tex}\frac{45}{100}{/tex} Now, we have, {tex}P(\frac {A}{ E_{1}})=\frac{1}{100}{/tex} {tex}P(\frac {A}{ E_{2}})=\frac{1.2}{100}{/tex} {tex}P(\frac {A}{E_{3}})=\frac{2}{100}{/tex} Using Bayes' theorem, we have, Required probability = {tex}P(\frac {E_{1}} { A}){/tex} = {tex}\frac{P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(A / E_{1}\right)}{P\left(E_{1}\right) P\left(A / E_{1}\right)+P\left(E_{2}\right) P\left(A / E_{2}\right)+P\left(E_{3}\right) P\left(A / E_{3}\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\frac{30}{100} \times \frac{1}{100}}{\frac{30}{100} \times+\times \frac{1}{100} \frac{25}{100} \frac{1.2}{100}+\frac{45}{100} \times \frac{2}{100}}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{30}{30+30+90}=\frac{30}{150}{/tex} = 0.2 4 Case Study Based Question 14. Two motorcycles A and B are running at the speed more than the allowed speed on the roads represented by the lines {tex}\vec{r}=\lambda(\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}){/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{r}}=(3 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}){/tex} {tex}+\mu(2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} respectively. Find the shortest distance between the given lines. (2) Find the point at which the motorcycles may collide. (2) Solution: Let {tex}\overrightarrow{a_1}=0,\overrightarrow{a_2}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k,\overrightarrow{b_2}=2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}=3\widehat i+3\widehat j{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=\begin{vmatrix}\widehat i&\widehat j&\widehat k\\1&2&-1\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}=3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k{/tex} {tex}\left(a_2-a_1\right)\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)\left(3\widehat i-3\widehat j-3\widehat k\right){/tex} = 9 - 9 = 0 Shortest distance between line is {tex}d=\left|\frac{\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right).\left(\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right)}{\left|\overrightarrow{b_1}\times\overrightarrow{b_2}\right|}\right|{/tex} {tex}d=\left|\frac0{3\sqrt3}\right|{/tex} = 0 units. From equation of both lines, we get {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\left(3\widehat i+3\widehat j\right)+\mu\left(2\widehat i+\widehat j+\widehat k\right){/tex} {tex}\lambda\widehat i+2\lambda\widehat j-\lambda\widehat k=\left(3+2\mu\right)\widehat i+\left(3+\mu\right)\widehat j+\mu\widehat k{/tex} On compairing {tex}\lambda=3+2\mu{/tex} ...(i) {tex}2\lambda=3+\mu{/tex} ...(ii) {tex}-\lambda=\mu{/tex} ...(iii) From eqn (iii) and (i) {tex}\mu=-1{/tex} From eqn (ii) and (iii) {tex}\lambda=1{/tex} {tex}\lambda\left(\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k\right)=\widehat i+2\widehat j-\widehat k{/tex} Hence, at point x = 1, y = 2 & z = -1 both motorcycle collides. 4
